title: Social Security and Medicare Trustees Reports
summary: This is a Treasury Department landing page describing the governance structure of the Social Security and Medicare Boards of Trustees. It provides no actual report data, projections, or substantive fiscal content—it merely describes administrative structure and links to the actual reports.
key_points: The Secretary of the Treasury serves as Chair of the Boards of Trustees for both Social Security and Medicare
The Boards consist of six trustees total: three federal officials (Social Security Commissioner, HHS Secretary, Labor Secretary) and two Presidentially-appointed Senate-confirmed public trustees
Annual trustees reports are submitted to Congress providing accounting of current and projected financial status of both trust funds
The page provides links to separate Social Security Report, Medicare Report, and a combined Summary, but the actual report content is not present in this source
why_it_matters: This source provides no actionable market or macro information. It is purely administrative metadata about report governance. The substantive fiscal sustainability data (trust fund depletion timelines, actuarial projections, cost growth rates) exists only in the linked reports, not this landing page.
macro_context: Annual Trustees Reports on Social Security and Medicare solvency are significant fiscal policy documents that could impact Treasury yields, fiscal deficit expectations, and long-term economic projections. However, this landing page contains none of that data—only the governance structure of who produces those reports.
market_structure_context: Not applicable. This page does not contain market-relevant data or analysis. The actual trustees reports (not this navigation page) would be relevant for understanding fiscal sustainability risks to Treasury market dynamics.

issues_found: Source is a navigation_page (landing page) - violates Rule 6 which mandates rejection for navigation, homepage, listing, or search pages
No published_at date provided - cannot assess source recency
Source contains no substantive report data, projections, or actuarial findings - only governance structure description
event_type set to 'unknown' - while understandable given thin content, the record lacks actionable categorization
Source is purely administrative metadata linking to actual reports, not an archiveable research document
Cannot verify relevance to market structure or macro catalysts without the actual trustees report content (trust fund depletion dates, solvency projections, cost growth rates)
